title: License changes to GPL-3.0-only for future releases
summary: Kage is switching future releases to GPL 3.0 only. GPL allows commercial use and selling, but distributed modified versions/derivatives must keep source available under GPL. README and npm metadata should say GPL 3.0 onl
body: Kage is switching future releases to GPL-3.0-only. GPL allows commercial use and selling, but distributed modified versions/derivatives must keep source available under GPL. README and npm metadata should say GPL-3.0-only, with a note that older MIT releases remain under their original terms.

context.fact: Future Kage releases use GPL-3.0-only while older MIT releases keep their original terms.
context.why: The project wants source-available copyleft terms for distributed modified versions while preserving prior release licensing.
context.trigger: Recall when editing LICENSE, README, npm package metadata, changelog, or release notes.
context.action: State GPL-3.0-only for future releases and avoid rewriting older MIT release terms.
context.verification: Repo-local capture grounded to LICENSE, README.md, mcp/package.json, mcp/README.md, and CHANGELOG.md.
context.risk_if_forgotten: Agents may publish inconsistent licensing metadata or imply that old MIT artifacts were relicensed.
context.stale_when: The project changes its license policy or legal review supersedes this decision.
